attributes.description:
Controls the height of the checkbox box (the square input element that users click to check/uncheck). This determines how tall the actual checkbox appears visually. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-height

Controls the width of the checkbox box (the square input element that users click to check/uncheck). This determines how wide the actual checkbox appears visually. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-width

Controls the size of the checkbox label text (the descriptive text that appears next to the checkbox). This text explains what the checkbox option represents, such as 'I agree to terms' or 'Subscribe to newsletter'.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-label-font-size

Sets the font family (typeface) for the checkbox label text. This determines the visual style of the text that describes what the checkbox option represents.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-label-font-family

Sets the text color of the checkbox label (the descriptive text that appears next to the checkbox). This determines what color the label text appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-label-color

Controls the horizontal spacing between the checkbox box (the square input element) and the label text. This creates visual separation so users can easily distinguish between the clickable checkbox and its descriptive text.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-label-margin-left

Sets the background color of the checkbox box (the square input element). When transparent, only the border is visible. When filled with a color, the checkbox appears solid. This affects the visual appearance of the unchecked state.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-background-color

Sets the color of the checkmark icon (the ✓ symbol) that appears inside the checkbox when it's checked. This is typically a contrasting color to ensure the checkmark is clearly visible against the checkbox background.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-icon-color

Controls the size of the checkmark icon (the ✓ symbol) that appears inside the checkbox when it's checked. This icon is typically smaller than the checkbox box itself to fit nicely inside. Acceptable units: px.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-icon-size

Controls the thickness of the border around the checkbox box (the square input element). This creates the outline that defines the checkbox shape and makes it visible to users.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-border-width

Controls the corner rounding of the checkbox box (the square input element). Small values create slightly rounded corners, while larger values make the checkbox more rounded or even circular.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-border-radius

Sets the color of the border around the checkbox box (the square input element). This determines the color of the outline that defines the checkbox shape in its default/unchecked state.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-border-color

Sets the background color of the checkbox box when it's checked. This fills the checkbox with a color to visually indicate that the option has been selected.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-checked-background-color

Sets the border color of the checkbox box when it's checked. This changes the outline color to match the checked state, creating a cohesive visual appearance.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-checked-border-color

Sets the color of the checkmark icon (✓) when the checkbox is checked. This ensures the checkmark is clearly visible against the colored background of the checked checkbox.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-checked-icon-color

Controls the size of the checkbox label text when the checkbox is checked. This text explains what the checkbox option represents, such as 'I agree to terms' or 'Subscribe to newsletter'.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-checked-label-font-size

Sets the font family (typeface) for the checkbox label text when the checkbox is checked. This determines the visual style of the text that describes what the checkbox option represents.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-checked-label-font-family

Sets the text color of the checkbox label when the checkbox is checked. This determines what color the label text appears in when the checkbox is selected.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-checked-label-color

Controls the overall transparency of the checkbox component when it is disabled. When set to 0.8, the component appears semi-transparent to indicate it cannot be interacted with.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-disabled-opacity

Controls the size of the checkbox label text when the checkbox is disabled. This text explains what the checkbox option represents, such as 'I agree to terms' or 'Subscribe to newsletter'.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-disabled-label-font-size

Sets the font family (typeface) for the checkbox label text when the checkbox is disabled. This determines the visual style of the text that describes what the checkbox option represents.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-disabled-label-font-family

Sets the text color of the checkbox label when the checkbox is disabled. This determines what color the label text appears in when the checkbox is disabled.
CSS variable: --wm-checkbox-disabled-label-color
